description: Partial state update — status changed but related fields not updated
diff:
--- a/src/subscription.py
+++ b/src/subscription.py
@@ -15,8 +15,7 @@ class Subscription:
     def renew(self):
         self.status = 'active'
         self.start_date = datetime.now()
-        self.end_date = self.start_date + timedelta(days=30)
-        self.payment_due = self.calculate_payment()
+        # end_date and payment will be set by the billing job

mock_response.result: {"review_status":"completed","pass":false,"issues":[{"check":"Partial state update — end_date and payment_due not set during renew","verdict":"renew() sets status=active and start_date but defers end_date and payment to an external billing job, creating a window where the subscription is active with no end_date","fix":"Set end_date and payment_due synchronously, or add a transitional status like 'renewing' until billing job completes","severity":"critical","confidence":"high","category":"correctness","file":"src/subscription.py","line_hint":"L17-L20","evidence":["status set to 'active' at L17 — subscription is immediately considered active","end_date assignment removed at L18 — active subscription has no expiry","comment says billing job will set these, but no guarantee of timing or success"],"what_would_falsify_this":"If the billing job runs synchronously before renew() returns, or if code downstream never reads end_date for active subscriptions","suggested_validation":"Check if any code path reads end_date when status is active — if so, this is a real gap","abstain_condition":""}],"summary":"Partial state update leaves active subscription without end_date"}
